note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1. The front of this CME is faint and difficult to track in GOES CCOR-1 and unable to be tracked in SOHO LASCO C3.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as potential source location.
A very approximate analysis based on the fit of this faint fuzzy front in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs. No source has been found in EUV imagery to confirm parameters.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2) and not confirmed by source (which has not been found).
Very uncertain analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). The longitude could be lower (~135 deg.)
Leading edge measurement mainly relying on imagery in STEREO A COR2A but with SOHO LASCO C2 data up to 2025-09-18T16:24Z.
Leading edge measurement using STEREO A COR2 and SOHO C2 for triangulation; some eastward deflection was noted in the leading edge relative to the supposed source.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 using later frames only, as the CME front shape widens and changes shape in early COR2A and LASCO C2 frames.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 with early frames as the diffuse front is difficult to track beyond this.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs. No clear source has been found for this CME.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). No source has been found to confirm parameters.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit of the faint fuzzy front with 3 coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). No source found to confirm these parameters.

A very uncertain analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). No source has been found for this CME in available EUV imagery to confirm this analysis.
Updated measurement including STEREO A COR2A imagery.
Very approximate  Analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). There seems to be a bit of a westward deflection from the source location.
Updated measurement with additional imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As the source is likely far-sided, a precise source location is unknown. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and COR2A.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. There is no GOES CCOR-1 imagery for this event due to an eclipse.
Single spacecraft 3D measurement with SOHO LASCO C2/C3 only due to no visibility in STEREO A COR2, using the observed source near S15W52 as the basis and finding a good fit at these parameters.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Due to uncertainty in the source location, the measured parameters may vary slightly.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The measured parameters may vary slightly due to uncertainty in the source location.

Best fit measurement using candidate source location, two frames of SOHO LASCO C2, and one frame STEREO A COR2. There is some uncertainty with this measurement due to the faintness of the leading edge and a data gap in SOHO LASCO lasting from 2025-09-24T09:24Z to 2025-09-24T12:00Z.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measured longitude based on the source location. The measured parameters may vary slightly due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event, preventing a stereoscopic measurement from being made.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2). No source has been found for this CME in available EUV imagery.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs and confirmed by the source seen in GOES SUVI and STEREO A EUV imagery near S17W75Z. Note that GOES CCOR-1 was not producing a good fit.

Somewhat speculative analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1), however since the CME is very faint and no clear source has been found for it we have no certainty that the narrow faint jet front we see S in STEREO A COR2 is the same jet we see SW in two other coronagraphs.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Best fit measurement using source location seen in UV imagery with some southeastward deflection, several frames of SOHO LASCO C2, one frame of SOHO LASCO C3, and one frame of STEREO A COR2. Leading edge becomes too diffuse to track a few frames into the SOHO LASCO C3 field of view.

Analysis based on the fit of this narrow jet with SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 and in alignment with the possible source - a C class flare from AR14232. No good fit with this analysis for CCOR-1.
Analysis based on the fit with SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs, confirming AR 14232 as a likely source.
Corrected analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2).

Analysis including both the southern and the northern lobe of this CME and based on a fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2). GOES CCOR-1 coronagraph (specifically the northern lobe of this CME as seen from CCOR-1) does not fit with this analysis.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs.  No source has been found for this CME.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Later analysis based on a good fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2), also fitting GOES CCOR-1. This analysis is confirmed by the location of the CME source (M class flare from AR 14232), however there seems to be some eastward deflection from the location of this Active Region, based on the fit with coronagraphs.
Analysis of this follow-up front is based solely on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.  No source has been found for this CME in available EUV imagery.

Approximate/speculative analysis based on the fit with SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs and fitting a bit to the east of AR 14232 which is a possible source, so perhapse there is a deflection to the east because of a coronal hole to the west of the Active Region.
Single spacecraft measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery, SOHO LASCO C3 is not as clearly seen in SOHO LASCO C3 due to control arm.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. An Earth eclipse prevents the use of GOES CCOR-1 during this analysis.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs, SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.  This analysis is confirmed by the location of the likely CME source - M1.0 flare from AR 14232.
3D measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. Event is too faint to be tracked in SOHO LASCO C3 or GOES CCOR-1.

This analysis includes a brighter, more southern lobe and a more faint northern lobe, both of which are better seen in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 coronagraphs. If the source of this CME is indeed a flare and filament eruption from AR 14232, this analysis suggests a significant SE deflection from the source.
Single spacecraft measurement based on source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2. Event fades before exiting the filed-of-view in SOHO LASCO C2.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. This event is too faint to track in GOES CCOR-1.
BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Approximate analysis of this very faint (especially in STEREO A COR2) front with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2) and based on the location of the source flare.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As the source is far-sided and a precise source location is unknown, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The measured longitude varies slightly from the source longitude, indicating possible deflection from a southwestern coronal hole.

Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 with a higher degree of uncertainty. This is due to the lack of clean observed source, faint leading edge in SOHO LASCO, a large data gap in STEREO A COR2 spanning most of this event, and the lobed front which is partially chopped off for this analysis focusing on the main equatorial lobe best viewed from COR2A. While uncertainty lies in the latitude, longitude, and half width, there is higher certainty in the speed being <=250 km/s.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and 2 available frames of STEREO A COR2 before its data gap. CME front diffuses out in LASCO C2 and is tricky to track in later frames and in LASCO C3.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Single spacecraft measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based off of approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, with some difficulty due to faintness of the CME front.
Triangulation between STEREO A COR2 with better visibility, and faint observations in SOHO LASCO C2. Source could range W150-W160.
Leading edge measurement out to nine solar radii when the CME becomes so diffuse it is unable to be measured.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

Best fit measurement of extremely faint leading edge using a few frames of SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1, before the leading edge becomes completely diffuse in all three coronagraphs. The leading edge becomes diffuse before reaching the SOHO LASCO C3 field of view.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Leading edge measurement using triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 to approximate the longitude; the measurement suggests some westward deflection relative to the source.
Measurement based on source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on source location with the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT. The CME is very faint in SOHO LASCO C2 and is not clearly measurable in SOHO LASCO C3.
Measurement based on the source location and the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. The CME appears to deflect to the west of the source location.
Very uncertain, approximate analysis with SOHO LASCO, based on the approximate source location - a large filament eruption very approximately centered at (S15W100).

A very approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs - SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2. The fit confirms that the source is far-sided, however STEREO A COR2 imagery is very fuzzy, so this analysis is somewhat speculative.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs. The source is clearly far-sided.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs. The source is clearly far-sided.
Measurement based on the approximate source location, accounting for deflection from a nearby coronal hole, and the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Very uncertain one-coronagraph (STEREO A COR2) analysis of this CME based on the flare in AR 4246 as the (rather speculative) source of this CME. Because the front is not detectable in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 imagery and the CME overlaps with two other CMEs, and due to the fact that the flare is accompanied by a very complex-shape dimming this analysis is very approximate.

Measurement based on source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The leading edge is not clearly measurable in SOHO LASCO C3 or GOES CCOR-1, and this measurement is based on limited imagery. Due to the faintness of the leading edge, this measurement is more approximate than usual.
Measurement with updated imagery based on SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.
Measurement based primarily on GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery, with one frame of SOHO LASCO C3 imagery available.
Measurement based on best-fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Analysis based on the fit of the front with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2) and somewhat confirmed by the approximate location of the post-eruptive arcades (N25E70).

Analysis of this (secondary, likely bulk) bright front following the possible shock front, based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2), confirmed by the approximate location of the post-eruptive arcades following the filament eruption.

Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, with greater uncertainty in all paramters due to the faint diffuse leading edge, but high confidence in the high northern latitude range out of the ecliptic. Half width barely 10 degrees, may be less.
Very approximate reanalysis of this front with more imagery based on two coronagraph fit: SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2, using later frames past ~04:00Z and not including LASCO C2 due to some observed acceleration when fitting all frames. Solid 3D fit around E110-E115.

One-coronagraph (SOHO) analysis based on the location of the possible source - a C4.3 flare from AR 14246. This source would imply a significant northern deflection, and some previous CMEs from this Active Region also deflected N. The CME is not seen in STEREO A COR2, likely because it is obscured by preceding, brighter CMEs to the NE.
BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 in SWPC_CAT as well as the source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery as well as source location.
Leading edge measurement of the CME assuming the source is located around W50 before it is overtaken by partial halo CME 2025-10-15T23:15Z to the northwest in all coronagraphs.
Preliminary measurement based on primarily GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT, with a few available frames of STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2/C3 used as well.
Leading edge measurement between STEREO and SOHO which showed some level of moderate deflection to the east relative to the source location of the eruption.
Three-spacecraft leading edge measurement to triangulate the source longitude.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement using candidate source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit using three frames of SOHO LASCO C2. Leading edge becomes diffuse after a few frames of SOHO LASCO C2.
Measurement using source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement using source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.

Parameters based on the potential source location as well as the best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. STEREO A COR2 was not used in this measurement due to limited frames and reliability on size and directionality of CME based on SOHO and GOES imagery.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs, SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 and somewhat confirming the possible source - filament eruption near the WSW limb.
Measurement using candidate source location seen in UV imagery and best fit in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. The leading edge is very faint and uncertain, particularly in STEREO A COR2, which adds uncertainty to this measurement.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2) and confirming the M1.5 flare from AR 14246 as the likely source.
Measurement using source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement using best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1, and consideration of candidate source location seen in available UV imagery.

Parameters based on best fit and potential source location. An initial diffuse outflow type front begins around 2025-10-18T15:48Z, however it does not have a well defined front and so it was excluded from the measurement.
Parameters are based on best fit. This CME is very diffuse in appearance which may impact these parameters.
Parameters are based on best fit. This CME is very diffuse in appearance which may impact these parameters.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery before it is overtaken by the CME first seen at 2025-10-19T00:36Z in SOHO LASCO C2 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Very approximate analysis based on the fit of the faint front with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). There are numerous consecutive fronts (well seen in C2), so any fit is somewhat speculative, assuming we are fitting the same front,
Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). No clear source has not been found.
Best-fit measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Best-fit measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and one frame from STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Parameters based on best fit of what is believed to be the BULK of CME.
Parameters based on what is believed to be the SHOCK of the CME.

Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 only and STEREO A COR2. Visiblity of this CME front in STEREO A COR2 is quite poor, this coupled with the high latiude puts higher variability on the longitude range W80-W110 (the expected source is far-sided, but triangulated on-disk with available imagery).
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. The CME is slightly wider in LASCO than COR2A, possibly due to viewing angle differences, so the width is determined from LASCO.

BULK triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. The thin half width, slightly northward deflection, and slightly different presentations in SOHO and STEREO coronagraphs cause some uncertainty in perfectly tracking the leading edge width and lat/lon, but high confidence in the out-of-the-ecliptic latitude.
SHOCK triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 of the much more faint, wider feature tracking just ahead of the thin leading edge.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
BULK triangulation between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
SHOCK triangulation between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement using source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.

Measurement of SHOCK using source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. Not captured in real time by STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2025-10-21T19:38Z.

Measurement of BULK using source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. Not captured in real time by STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2025-10-21T19:38Z.
Measurement using best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Updated measurement of the leading edge using the two frames of STEREO A COR2 which have become available and several frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and C3.

Measurement of the SHOCK using the location of the candidate source seen in available UV imagery and best fit between a handful of frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. Not captured in real time by STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2025-10-22T19:23Z.

Measurement of the BULK using the location of the candidate source seen in available UV imagery and best fit between a handful of frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. Not captured in real time by STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2025-10-22T19:23Z.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEROE A COR2 in SWPC_CAT. The CME was not clearly measurable in GOES CCOR-1 running difference imagery.
Measurement based on source location as well as best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement using limited SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery due to the CME's faintness.
Updated measurement with additional imagery in SWPC_CAT.

This measurement is speculative as this event is difficult to observe in SOHO LASCO C2 imagery due to the faintness and overlapping material from preceding events. However, this measurement is based on the approximate source location and best fit between STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement of the brighter bulk feature based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

SHOCK measurement based on the source longitude. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event, the shock front associated with this event is not seen in STEREO A. The measured parameters may vary as a result.

Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, although with some slight uncertainty due to the leading edge faintness in all coronagraph imagery. CME starts <10 degree half width but expands to fit in later imagery.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, although with some slight uncertainty due to the leading edge faintness in all coronagraph imagery. CME <10 degree half width.

Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The leading edge of this event becomes too diffuse to measure after a few frames. The measurement relies on early imagery as a result.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, with some uncertainty in parameters due to the faint, diffuse leading edge in all coronagraphs.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs and somewhat confirmed by the (very minor) potential source, a minor filament eruption.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1).

Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1) and confirming former AR 14246 located at -174 deg longitude at the time of eruption as the source.

Updated SHOCK analysis triangulation between STEREO A COR2, SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and GOES CCOR-1. Due to the oblong shape of the shock feature, the front does not fit well in all 3 coronagraphs together. STEREO A COR2 has the better viewing angle of the front with its spacecraft position around W49, so this fit focuses on capturing the full front in COR2A and fits most of the front, focusing on the eastern side, in LASCO C2/C3 and CCOR-1.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Single spacecraft measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.
BULK measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR and STEREO A COR2.
SHOCK measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1); with no support from the coronal signatures since the CME is clearly far-sided.

Very speculative measurement with one coronagraph only (since STEREO A COR2 is not available because of the large data gap). The CME latitude could be different: the latitude of +24 deg has been selected as the latitude of the former AR 14246, however this AR has produced many CMEs with much higher latitude. As a result, latitude and speed of this event could be significantly different.

A rather speculative one-coronagraph (LASCO) analysis because the CME is not seen in STEREO coronagraph due to a large data gap. The latitude (and hence the speed) could be different. The latitude of 24 deg is chosen to correspond to the latitude of former AR 14246 when it was seen on the front side. The Active Region produced multiple more northern CMEs, so this analysis is very approximate.
Very approximate, uncertain one-coronagraph measurement of a fainter wider shock.
Approximate one coronagraph analysis (SOHO) due to data gap in STEREO A and likely far-sidedness of this CME.
Single spacecraft measurement based on approximate source location.
BULK Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

Single spacecraft 3D measurement with SOHO LASCO C2/C3 using the central point of the filament eruption, N20E41, as a starting point and adjusting latitude and width to fit. Longitude could range E54-E27.

Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 finding deflection away from the observed source closer to the limb, but the faint and diffuse nature of the front makes tracking it beyond earliest frames difficult, so larger uncertainty exists on the longitude with high confidence in the far northern latitude range.

Measurement using the best fit between an abundance of frames of SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1. Source is not visible in available UV imagery due to current lack of viewpoint coverage for beyond the northeast limb of the Earth-facing disk.
SHOCK measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement using triangulated best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best bit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
Leading edge/bulk measurement using GOES SUVI, STEREO A COR2, and SOHO LASCO C2/C3 out to about 23 solar radii.
Shock measurement primarily relying on STEREO Ahead as the CME was not able to be fit in two coronagraphs simultaneously.
Measurement using both SOHO and STEREO A coronagraphs and knowledge of the source location.
Updated measurement using more frames of SOHO C2/C3.
Preliminary measurement using SOHO C2 and STEREO A COR2A.
Measurement using source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Leading edge measurement using SOHO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES to triangulate the longitude of the CME out to 8 solar radii.
Leading edge measurement using three spacecraft (SOHO, GOES, and STEREO A) for triangulation of the longitude.

Measurement using best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1. Source is likely beyond the NE limb of the Earth-facing disk and is not visible in available UV imagery of the disk.
Measurement using best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement using source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
BULK analysis, triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO COR2A, and GOES CCOR-1.

SHOCK analysis, triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The oblong shock shape is difficult to align between all 3 coronagraphs, including partial occulation by the LASCO C3 pylon, so a best fit is done to capture the width in COR2A cutting off some dimpled front shapes to fit the width in LASCO C2/C3 and CCOR-1.

Measurement using candidate source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of STEREO A COR2 and a few frames from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The leading edge is partially obstructed by the pylon in both SOHO LASCO C3 and in GOES CCOR-1. The leading edge is most clearly seen in STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between GOES CCOR-1, STEREO A COR2, and two available SOHO LASCO C3 frames.

Approximate analysis based on the location of the CME source (filament on/behind the SW limb in STEREO A EUV 304 and on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1).
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2). No source was found for this CME.
Approximate  analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). The CME is far-sided.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). The source for this CME was not found.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1) and confirmed by the source (flare and eruption) that is likely just behind the NE limb.

Measurement using candidate source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1. Leading edge becomes too diffuse to track about one forth of the way through the SOHO LASCO C3 FOV.

Measurement using source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. Leading edge is too diffuse to reliably track in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.

Parameters based on best fit using STEREO A COR2, GOES CCOR-1, and SOHO LASCO C3 imagery. The lower left hand boundary of the CME appears to move faster than the northern portion of the CME. It's possible with the long duration eruption associated with this CME that there could be more than one feature included in the measurement, however the parameters above include the entire feature to the NE with emphasis on the lower left hand boundary rather than the slightly slower northern right hand boundary.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). There seems to be deflection westward from the source that is on/behind the E limb.

Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). If the source is minor activity in a plage centered around (S10E40) then there is a significant deflection to the E.

Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). A possible source could be minor eruption in a plage centered around (S10E35), but this source would mean a significant deflection of this CME eastward.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 ); the source has not been found and is likely far-sided.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. This event is not measurable in GOES CCOR-1 due to an eclipse.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

These parameters are based on the best fit of the BULK of the CME using GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Some deflection may have occurred with the nearby negative polarity coronal hole feature visible on the Earth-facing solar disk.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
These parameters are based on the best fit using SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). The source for this event has not been found.

Final analysis with more imagery. It is based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1) and on the source location of the M3.5 flare. This complex event associated with two flares is treated as a single CME.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. This event is not measurable in GOES CCOR-1 due to an eclipse period.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.

BULK analysis, triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 of the bright bulk feature observed primarily to the NE in all coronagraphs. Measurable SOHO LASCO C2 imagery not yet available due to a downlink gap.

SHOCK analysis, best fit triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 of the faint, wider halo shock in all 3 coronagraphs. Due to the large width of the observed shock front and viewing angle differences between the coronagraphs, this best fit is made using the width in GOES CCOR-1/SOHO LASCO C3 and tracking the edge of the front with less emphasis on the width in COR2A. Measurable SOHO LASCO C2 imagery not yet available due to a downlink gap.

Triangulated measurement between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2; SOHO LASCO C2/C3 not yet available due to ongoing downlink gap. The CME front is somewhat irregularly shaped, as such this triangulation captures the majority of the bulk material as best as possible.

Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2, tracking the irregular diffuse bulk material with a best fit as possible, finding a good fit near the flare source region (N25E36).
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Best-fit measurement based on limited imagery from STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Best-fit measurement of the SHOCK feature based on running difference imagery from STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement of the inner, brighter bulk portion based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
SHOCK measurement of the faint, wider feature surrounding the bulk of this event. Measured parameters based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement using source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1. Analysis of this CME was affected by a data gap in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 lasting from 2025-11-06T01:54Z to 2025-11-06T06:30Z.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

Approximate analysis based on the fit with SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs. Two possible sources are further to east from the longitude of the fit and much further south, so there is likely a significant deflection to the NW.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1).
Approximate analysis based on the fit with SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs.
Measurement of the bright northeast bulk feature based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
SHOCK measurement of the fainter halo feature, which surrounds the brighter northeast bulk feature, based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit using STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C3 imagery. The CME initially measured less than 200 km/s but was reanalyzed with later frames and sped up significantly.
Leading edge measurement using triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2A.
Measurement of the shock front out to 13 solar radii.
Updated bulk measurement out to 25 solar radii.

Parameters based on best fit, however the feature continues to drift north in the STEREO A COR2 field of view and may have a more northern deflection in later frames not properly captured in this measurement.
BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on the best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Best-fit measurement based on GOES CCOR-1 and limited imagery from STEREO A COR2.

Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs indicates the source should be behind the NW limb, however no source has been found in STEREO A EUV imagery to confirm this fit. Because of the extreme faintness of the CME and the lack of coronal signatures these parameters are rather speculative.

This analysis was made with one coronagraph based on the location of C6.1 flare from Active Region 14274 as one potential source. However, there is no eruption seen in association with the flare. The CME is not seen in STEREO A coronagraph, so this analysis is rather speculative.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement using best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 and the candidate source location seen in available UV imagery.
Measurement of the SHOCK feature using the best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement of the BULK using source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement using candidate source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement using source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement using source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Leading edge measurement using triangulation between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2A.
Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.

Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1. There is some uncertainty in this measurement due to deflection within the FOV. The CME also appears to accelerate about half-way through the FOV of SOHO LASCO C3.
Best-fit measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.

Approximate, one-coronagraph (SOHO LASCO C2) analysis based on the location of AR 4274. Not confirmed by STEREO A coronagraph as the CME is fully covered by the data gap starting at 2025-11-15T11:40Z.

A tentative analysis with one coronagraph (SOHO LASCO), based on a very approximate source location (N45W20). In the absence of STEREO A imagery due to long data gap this is the best that can be done.

Approximate analysis of the second front of this CME (likely its bulk) with one coronagraph analysis method (using SOHO) and based on the approximate location of the source - an erupting 30-40 long northern filament. STEREO A imagery is not available for this event.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2).
Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). No clear source was found for this CME.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1).
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

Measurement based on the approximate source longitude. A triangulated measurement was not available due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery during this event. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Updated analysis with more available imagery, measuring slightly less wide, triangulated between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2, as SOHO LASCO C2/C3 were in a data gap at time of measurement.
Two-coronagraph analysis of this CME with SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 imagery, confirmed by the location of the far-sided source (former Active Region 14274).

Approximate analysis based on a fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). The front is difficult to fit because of extensive data gaps in SOHO and STEREO A, because of complexity of the CME's front and its faintness in CCOR-1, which alone has a good coverage at this point.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on approximate source location using GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement using source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

Measurement using estimated source location based on signatures in available UV imagery and best fit between available frames of STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1. Not captured in real time by SOHO LASCO due to a scheduled data gap.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and one frame of STEREO A COR2 due to poor running difference COR2A image quality. Although no source is directly observed for this CME, it triangulates to the far-side via this fit.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between GOES CCOR-1, STEREO A COR2, and SOHO LASCO C2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Updated measurement using SOHO and GOES imagery. Parameters based on best fit with source location.

Parameters based on best fit of the BULK of the CME, however the fit is better in STEREO A COR2 imagery than in SOHO or GOES imagery due to triangulation limitations. The half-width appears too wide for the visible bulk feature in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery compared to the visible bulk feature in STEREO A COR2 imagery. This may be due to limited viewpoints of the full CME.
Parameters based on best fit of the SHOCK.
Parameters based on best fit. There is deflection of the CME in the STEREO A COR2 field of view likely due to proximity of nearby coronal hole.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Single spacecraft 3D analysis with only SOHO LASCO C2 at E68, as no other coronagraphs observe this CME and it fades after initial C2 frames. As such the longitude range could vary E60-E70.
Measurement based on the source location and the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement using source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Follow-up measurement using observations of source location seen beyond the limb in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.

Measurement using source location seen in available UV imagery and fit of the leading edge using several frames of GOES CCOR-1. This CME was not captured in real time by SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 due to regularly scheduled data gaps for each instrument.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. This event was not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Triangulation of the CME leading edge between STEREO A COR2A strongly suggests the source is far-sided with respect to the Earth-facing disk.
Measurement based on best-fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. GOES CCOR-1 was not used as a majority of the propagation of the CME was covered by a data gap.
Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement using approximate source location seen in available UV imagery and best fit between a few frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. The leading edge is extremely faint in STEREO A COR2 imagery, which adds uncertainty to this measurement.
Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement using approximate location of strong candidate source seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.

Measurement using approximated location of strong candidate source seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of STEREO A COR2 and a few frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement using approximate location of source seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

Measurement using approximated location of strong candidate source signature seen in available UV imagery and best fit between several frames of STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1. This CME was not captured in real time by SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a scheduled data gap in SOHO LASCO.
Measurement based on best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. GOES CCOR-1 imagery was limited due to missing frames during Earth-shine event.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2) and confirmed by possible source (faint post-eruptive arcades and dimming).
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Parameters based on best fit using GOES CCOR-1, STEREO A COR2, and SOHO LASCO C3 imagery. The CME feature in STEREO A COR2 imagery appears significantly more narrow than what is visible in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. This is possibly due to the different perspectives, but uncertainty of the parameters cannot be ignored.

Approximate analysis based on the fit of the front with SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs, confirmed by the possible location of the likely source (eruption of prominence off/behind the NE limb).
SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 analysis, with no STEREO A COR2 imagery available at measurement time the source longitude of E89 is used, and longitude could vary by E95-E80.

Very approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. If the source is the simultaneously going M1.5 class flare from AR 14294 near the SE limb, there must have been a significant deflection of the CME northward.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Approximate analysis based on the fit of the front in three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). The source is likely one of the lobes of what looks like a three-pronged prominence eruption from/behind the NE limb, seen starting at 2025-11-29T05:36Z in GOES SUVI 304.

Analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). The source is likely the southern lobe of what looks like a three-pronged prominence eruption from/behind the NE limb, seen starting at 2025-11-29T05:36Z in GOES SUVI 304.

Approximate/speculative analysis of the bulk front of this CME, based mainly on the fact that the CME is seen directly N in STEREO A COR2, indicating the longitude of -130 degrees and also 'confirmed' by the fit of the brighter front in COR2 with the much fainter front  to the NE in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1.

A very speculative analysis of the faint full halo shock front of this CME, based on the assumption that the longitude of the CME is approximately -130 degrees (since it is seen as full halo in STEREO A). However, there are multiple other fast CMEs happening simultaneously with this (including a faster 2025-11-29T05:48Z CME), so the shock may be associated with one of these other CMEs.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). No source has been found for this CME in EUV imagery.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. CME front is faint in white light but more noticable in running difference imagery.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

The CME front is lopsided due to the eruption deflection and partial overlap with CME 2025-11-29T16:00Z. Best attempt at triangulating and following the leading edge direction is taken between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2, which does miss some material on the northern edge that cannot fit in the swpc_cat tool's width due to the elongated CME shape, as such the half-width may be wider than 27 degrees.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Triangulation between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2, and two available frames of SOHO LASCO C2 before its downlink gap begins.

Updated bulk measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This measurement focuses on the bright portion of the CME and excludes the faint outer shock feature.
SHOCK measurement focusing on the faint feature surrounding the bulk portion. Measured parameters based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2). During this analysis SOHO LASCO imagery was not available.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2).

Measured longitude based on the approximate source location. As this event is not observed in STEREO A COR2 imagery, a triangulated measurement was not possible. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement based on the best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event is faint and likely a halo as observed by STEREO A, making
Very approximate analysis based on the fit of a very faint second halo in STEREO A COR2 with the rather messy follow-up to 2025-12-03T11:06Z CME, front seen in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2, finding a good fit with some deflected away from the observed flare source at N10E58.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. The CME distorts and expands from early frames in LASCO C2, so only later frames in C3 and COR2A are used when it takes its more final shape.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Leading edge measurement using only SOHO C2/C3 and knowledge of the source location to approximate the longitude.

Very speculative analysis based on the fit of the faint but clear loop-like front in STEREO A COR2 and the extremely faint fuzzy half-loop front that might be seen in SOHO LASCO C2 in a number of early timeframes to the NNE.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1) and supporting a minor eruption in AR 14298 as a likely source.

Approximate analysis based on the fit of this very narrow fronts in two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2). The source is behind the east limb and cannot confirm the longitude of the fit.
Very speculative analysis based on the fit of a second faint but clear loop front seen in STEREO A COR2 and the extremely faint fuzzy front that can be discerned in SOHO LASCO C2 in early timestamps.
Measurement using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, based on the potential source location. The CME is not measurable in STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on source location using SOHO LASCO C2 imagery only. The CME was not measurable in other running difference imagery.
Best-fit measurement based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 as well as probable source location seen in STA EUVI 304.
Best-fit measurement based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 as well as visible source.
Preliminary BULK measurement based on early frames from STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2.
SHOCK measurement based on triangulation of SHOCK feature among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Leading edge measurement using approximation of the source based on source EUV imagery
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on fit in STEREO A COR2 with visible source location.
Measurement based on best-fit of widest part of the bulk in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1 only analysis, as the CME travels into the LASCO C3 pylon and STEREO A COR2 is in a data gap. Using the flare source at N26W30 (AR 14299) as the source, could vary around W20-W40 and N20-N30.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2), if we are correct regarding the corresponding fronts.
Approximate analysis based on the fit of the faint front with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2). No source has been found in EUV imagery to confirm this analysis.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
Preliminary parameters based on best fit using GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1) and confirmed by the likely source centered at N35W35.

Initial, preliminary one-coronagraph (SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1) analysis of this CME based on the source location (S17W66) and on the limited imagery from CCOR-1 and two timestamps of C2 because of expected delay in SOHO imagery.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 with only early frames, before the CME front becomes too faint to resolve.
Triangulation between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 with only early frames, before the CME front becomes too faint to resolve.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. LASCO C3 cannot be used as the CME travels into the pylon.
Measurement based on approximate source location using GOES CCOR-1 imagery only in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the source location, accounting for visible southern deflection, and the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Parameters based on triangulation and best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The best fit does not seem to match the source location, however when trying to fit closer to the source the CME features did not match in SOHO/GOES and STEREO A perspectives.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 as well as visible source.
